steps.title: Match prices to variables
steps.content:
The equation is
$14x+15y=436$
The coefficient $14$ matches the short-sleeved shirt price, and the coefficient $15$ matches the long-sleeved shirt price. The variables count how many shirts of each type were sold.

steps.title: Eliminate price choices
steps.content: Since $x$ and $y$ are quantities, choices C and D are not right: they describe prices, but the prices are already the coefficients $14$ and $15$.

steps.title: Interpret the variable
steps.content: Because $15y$ represents total revenue from long-sleeved shirts, $y$ represents the number of long-sleeved shirts sold. Choice B would match $x$, the short-sleeved shirt count. The answer is A.
